ops.title: Entity-kind enumeration surfaces: where a new kind must land
ops.body:
Tiering policy (decided 2026-07-19): the **core record kinds** agents route work to are task, note, doc, log, papercut, runbook, investigation. **Sprint/project are the planning layer** — they stay out of one-line nudges and the record router by design; SKILL.md presents them as the optional layer. A surface may curate its *presentation*, never its *kind universe*.

A new kind (or record noun) isn't done at the Go layer. It lands in EVERY surface below in the same change:

## Prose surfaces
- `README.md` — commands-table row; a use case for headline kinds; the MCP paragraph's tool examples.
- `.claude/fragments/AGENTS.md/cc-notes-development-guide.fragment.md` — Repository Structure annotations (`model/`, `internal/cli`). AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md are GENERATED: render with the cc-guides version pinned in `.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock` (release binary, not a dev build) and commit fragments + artifacts together.
- cc-skills `plugin/guides/md/claude-rules.md` § Durable Records (renders into every fleet repo's CLAUDE.md) — add the routing clause, push cc-skills@main, re-render consumers.
- `plugin/skills/using-cc-notes/SKILL.md` — tools table, canonical flow, cheat-sheet; `references/cli-reference.md` (full per-kind section); `references/tasks-vs-notes.md` (the N-way count); `references/lifecycle-and-hygiene.md` (which lifecycle bucket the kind joins).

## Hook/nudge surfaces (plugin/hooks/)
- `session.py` `announce_cc_notes_available` — BOTH wording variants; the assertion in `tests/test_cc_notes.py` ("announce fires: names the durable tooling") pins the fallback line verbatim.
- `record.py` `RECORD_ROUTER_SYSTEM` + a routing branch; `common.py` `RECORD_KINDS` only if the kind uses the generic `record_command()` path (runbook/investigation precedent: dedicated branches instead).
- `redirect.py` `CC_NOTES_TOOLS`; `workflow.py` `CC_NOTES_WRITE_VERBS` + `MCP_READ_TOOLS`.
- `plugin/hooks/README.md` — teach table, record-router table, framework primitive list.
- `approval.py` — if the new surface reads/writes filesystem paths or executes anything, extend the DANGEROUS_* carve-outs (see doc 7b4a1647).

## Binary/manifest surfaces
- `internal/mcpserver/instructions.go` — hard cap 2KB (clients truncate); pay for additions with trims.
- `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` + `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` descriptions; bump the plugin version.
- `web/src/kinds.ts` (EntityKind union — the viz source of truth), `internal/cli/root.go` package doc, `internal/refs` roots map, a fold folder, `internal/mcpserver/tools_<kind>.go` (parity tests guard both directions).

Precedent: the 2026-07 sweep that synced all of the above to the 8-kind model (investigation was absent from README/AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/session.py five weeks after sprint/project shipped and two days after investigation did).
ops.when: adding a new entity kind, record noun, or agent-facing command; or auditing docs/guides/hooks for kind coverage
